description: Financial technology companies encompassing payments, lending, banking infrastructure, wealth management, insurance technology, and cryptocurrency/blockchain. Includes both consumer-facing fintech and B2B infrastructure providers serving banks and financial institutions.

pain_points.pain: Regulatory compliance burden consuming engineering and operational resources
pain_points.context: Multi-jurisdiction compliance (federal, state, international) requires dedicated teams and constant vigilance for new requirements

pain_points.pain: Fraud prevention while maintaining frictionless user experience
pain_points.context: Every fraud control adds friction that reduces conversion; finding balance is constant challenge
Security reviews, penetration testing, and compliance audits add weeks to release cycles

pain_points.pain: Legacy system integration with banks and financial institutions
pain_points.context: Partner banks run on decades-old mainframe systems with limited API capabilities and slow change processes

pain_points.pain: SOC 2 and security certification costs and timeline
pain_points.context: SOC 2 Type II takes 6-12 months and $50K+ to obtain, required for almost every enterprise deal

pain_points.pain: Vendor risk management burden from banking partners
pain_points.context: Bank partners require extensive due diligence, ongoing monitoring, and documentation that strains small teams
High chargeback rates threaten payment processing relationships and trigger account termination
Each country has different identity verification, sanctions screening, and suspicious activity reporting requirements

buying_triggers: Failed regulatory audit with 90-day remediation deadline
Fraud incident requiring immediate enhanced controls
New regulation deadline approaching (PCI DSS 4.0, DORA)
Bank partnership requiring SOC 2 and security documentation
Series A/B funding requiring compliance infrastructure
Geographic expansion requiring new jurisdiction compliance
Vendor unresponsive to compliance documentation requests
Competitor breach creating board-level security focus
budget_cycle: Enterprise fintech follows annual budget planning in Q4. Compliance and security spending often has separate budget from product development. Regulatory deadlines can trigger off-cycle emergency purchases. Bank partnership requirements bypass normal procurement when deal-critical.
regulatory_concerns: PCI DSS compliance for payment card handling
KYC (Know Your Customer) identity verification mandates
SEC regulations for securities and investment products
compliance_language: data encryption at rest and in transit

terminology.definition: Know Your Customer - the process of verifying customer identity using government IDs, biometrics, and documentation before allowing account opening or transactions
terminology.usage_context: Onboarding discussion. 'Our KYC flow needs to verify identity in under 2 minutes or we lose customers to competitors.'

terminology.definition: Anti-Money Laundering - compliance program to detect and prevent money laundering, terrorist financing, and other financial crimes through transaction monitoring and suspicious activity reporting
terminology.usage_context: Compliance infrastructure. 'Our AML program includes real-time transaction monitoring and quarterly SAR filing.'

terminology.definition: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard - mandatory security standard for any company handling credit card data, with 12 requirements covering encryption, access control, and monitoring
terminology.usage_context: Payment security. 'We need PCI DSS Level 1 certification before the card network will approve our application.'

terminology.definition: Sarbanes-Oxley Act - federal law requiring public companies to maintain internal controls over financial reporting, with implications for fintech companies handling financial data
terminology.usage_context: Enterprise compliance. 'Our bank partner requires SOX-compliant financial controls for any vendor touching their ledger.'

terminology.definition: Automated Clearing House - the US payment network for electronic bank-to-bank transfers, with next-day settlement for most transactions
terminology.usage_context: Payment rails. 'ACH is cheaper than cards but the 1-2 day settlement creates cash flow challenges for our customers.'

terminology.definition: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication - the global messaging network for international wire transfers between banks
terminology.usage_context: International payments. 'SWIFT transfers take 2-5 days and cost $25-50 in fees, which is why we're exploring blockchain alternatives.'

terminology.definition: Regulatory framework and technology allowing third-party access to bank account data and payment initiation through secure APIs, with customer consent
terminology.usage_context: Data access. 'Open Banking APIs let us pull transaction history directly from the bank instead of relying on screen scraping.'

terminology.definition: Banking as a Service - infrastructure that allows non-bank companies to offer banking products (accounts, cards, lending) through API integration with licensed bank partners
terminology.usage_context: Product enablement. 'Our BaaS partner handles the banking license and compliance so we can focus on the user experience.'

terminology.definition: Integration of financial services (payments, lending, insurance) directly into non-financial platforms and applications at the point of need
terminology.usage_context: Product strategy. 'Embedded finance lets us offer buy-now-pay-later right in the checkout flow without redirecting to a bank.'

terminology.definition: Regulatory Technology - software solutions that help financial institutions comply with regulations more efficiently through automation, monitoring, and reporting
terminology.usage_context: Compliance tooling. 'We use RegTech for automated suspicious activity monitoring instead of manual review.'

terminology.definition: Cryptocurrency designed to maintain stable value relative to a reference asset (usually USD), enabling crypto payments without price volatility
terminology.usage_context: Crypto payments. 'Stablecoins like USDC give us the blockchain rails without the Bitcoin volatility.'

terminology.definition: Decentralized Finance - blockchain-based financial services that operate without traditional intermediaries through smart contracts and decentralized protocols
terminology.usage_context: Crypto strategy. 'DeFi protocols offer higher yields but the regulatory status is uncertain.'

terminology.definition: Traditional Finance - conventional financial institutions and systems (banks, brokerages, exchanges) as contrasted with DeFi and crypto-native alternatives
terminology.usage_context: Industry positioning. 'We're bridging TradFi and crypto by offering regulated custody with DeFi yield access.'

terminology.definition: The authoritative record of all financial transactions, typically using double-entry accounting where every transaction has matching debit and credit entries
terminology.usage_context: System architecture. 'Our ledger system uses double-entry accounting and immutable audit trails for reconciliation.'

terminology.definition: A disputed credit card transaction where the cardholder's bank reverses the charge, typically costing the merchant the transaction amount plus fees
terminology.usage_context: Risk management. 'Our chargeback rate hit 1.2% and the payment processor is threatening to terminate our account.'

common_objections.objection: Are you SOC 2 Type II certified?
common_objections.underlying_concern: This is a binary gate. Without SOC 2 Type II, enterprise customers and bank partners will not proceed. No exceptions, no workarounds.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: If yes: Provide report immediately (one-click access, not 'contact sales'). If in progress: Share timeline and interim bridge letter. If no: Be honest - this will end most enterprise conversations until certification is complete.

common_objections.objection: What happens if you get breached? Who pays for the regulatory fines?
common_objections.underlying_concern: FinTech companies face cascading liability - if a vendor gets breached, the fintech customer may face CFPB/FCA fines. Need clear liability boundaries and indemnification.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Lead with incident response plan (documented, tested). State breach notification SLA (24-48 hours). Show cyber liability insurance coverage. Discuss indemnification terms openly - most vendors cap liability, so be honest about boundaries.

common_objections.objection: We need to run this through our vendor risk assessment process
common_objections.underlying_concern: Regulators require documented vendor risk management. Every vendor must complete security questionnaires, provide compliance documentation, and undergo ongoing monitoring.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Provide vendor risk assessment toolkit proactively: SOC 2 report, penetration test results, security questionnaire responses, insurance certificates. Offer to work with their compliance team directly.

common_objections.objection: How does this integrate with our core banking system?
common_objections.underlying_concern: Bank partners run on legacy mainframes (often 30+ years old) with limited API capabilities. Integration projects can take months and require bank IT resources that are always overcommitted.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show existing integrations with their specific core banking provider. Provide technical architecture documentation. Offer dedicated integration support. Set realistic timeline expectations (months, not weeks).

common_objections.objection: Our compliance team needs to review the contract terms
common_objections.underlying_concern: Financial services contracts require extensive legal review for liability, indemnification, data handling, and regulatory compliance terms. This process cannot be rushed.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Accept timeline reality (weeks to months for legal review). Provide redlined contract templates upfront. Be prepared to negotiate - fintech legal teams have specific requirements that may differ from your standard terms.

common_objections.objection: What's your audit trail and data retention policy?
common_objections.underlying_concern: Regulators require 5-7 year data retention and complete audit trails for all transactions. If vendor can't prove this, fintech fails their regulatory examination.
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Document specific retention periods (minimum 5 years, 7 years for certain transaction types). Show immutable audit log capabilities. Explain data export and portability for regulatory requests.

switching_barriers: SOC 2 audit of new vendor required before migration
Bank partner approval needed for any infrastructure change
Regulatory notification requirements for vendor changes
Historical transaction data and audit trail continuity
Integration complexity with core banking systems
Staff training and process documentation updates

scenario_templates.template: You're a {role} at a {company_size} payments company. Your bank partner just informed you they need SOC 2 documentation within 60 days or they'll terminate the relationship. {trigger_event}. You need to solve {pain_point} before the deadline.

You're a {role} at a {company_size} lending startup. A regulator just issued a 90-day remediation order after finding gaps in your AML program. {trigger_event}. Fixing {pain_point} is now your top priority before the follow-up exam.

You're a {role} at a {company_size} neobank. Your fraud losses doubled last quarter and the board is demanding immediate action. {trigger_event}. You must address {pain_point} without adding friction that hurts conversion.

You're a {role} at a {company_size} crypto company preparing for Series B. Investors are requiring enterprise-grade compliance before they'll close. {trigger_event}. You need to demonstrate {pain_point} is solved in 30 days.

You're a {role} at a {company_size} BaaS provider. A major fintech customer is threatening to leave because your API documentation is outdated and integration support is slow. {trigger_event}. You need to address {pain_point} to save a $500K annual contract.
